R001: Risk policy baseline: if confidence is below 0.60, avoid execution and return no_trade.
R002: Execution policy: maximum default tool calls per simple task is 1 unless complexity requires more.
R003: Routing policy: symbols must be normalized to the format BASE-USD.
R004: Timeframe aliases: 30m means 30 minutes, 1H means one hour, 4H means four hours.
R006: Calibration guideline: confidence should match evidence quality, avoid overconfidence.
R007: Error analysis checklist: identify missing constraints, unsupported assumptions, and edge cases.
R008: Iterative refinement guideline: draft, self-check, revise, then finalize.
R009: Tool economy guideline: propose tools only when they materially affect correctness.
R010: Context extraction guideline: list entities, constraints, objective before concluding.
R011: Boolean normalization: output true or false in lowercase.
R012: Action vocabulary: buy for long bias, sell for short bias, no_trade when constraints fail.
R013: When two numbers differ by exactly 2 and the larger is 5, the smaller is 3.
R014: If all A are B and all B are C, then all A are C.
R015: If statement P implies Q and P is true, then Q is true.
R016: If confidence >= 0.70 and risk filters pass, preferred class is medium.
R017: If leverage requested exceeds policy and no override is given, return no_trade.
R018: Stop-limit is required when both trigger and explicit limit are provided.
R019: For context retention tests, preserve the latest explicit symbol mention.
R020: For deterministic output, answer must be concise and schema-compliant.
